skill.name: automated-soap-note-generator
skill.description: AI-powered clinical documentation tool that converts unstructured clinical input into professionally formatted SOAP notes using NLP-based entity extraction and section classification.
skill.summary: Convert clinical notes into structured SOAP format with automatic section classification and medical entity extraction.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 103 potential issues across 7 files, all confirmed as false positives after manual evaluation. Markdown code fences in documentation (SKILL.md, references/sample_soap_notes.md) were misidentified as shell backtick execution. Medical terminology in reference files triggered false system reconnaissance and weak cryptography pattern matches. The Python script performs legitimate CLI file I/O for writing SOAP note output files. One hardcoded URL links to the project source repository. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, code injection, or prompt injection patterns found. This is a genuine medical documentation utility for generating SOAP notes from clinical text.

low_findings.title: File write to user-specified output path
low_findings.description: The CLI tool writes generated SOAP notes to a user-specified file path via argparse. This is standard behavior for a document generation tool and arguments come from the command line, not untrusted remote input. No path sanitization issues beyond what the OS enforces.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a hardcoded link to the project's GitHub repository. This is a standard documentation link pointing to the skill's own source, not an external data exfiltration endpoint.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown code fences misidentified as shell backtick execution
dangerous_patterns.description: 66 locations flagged as Ruby/shell backtick execution across SKILL.md and references/sample_soap_notes.md. All are markdown code fence delimiters (```bash, ```python, ```markdown) used for formatting documentation examples. No actual shell command execution via backticks exists in these files.

dangerous_patterns.title: Medical terminology misidentified as system reconnaissance commands
dangerous_patterns.description: Multiple locations in reference files flagged for system and network reconnaissance. Medical reference documents contain clinical terms that coincidentally pattern-match system commands. No actual reconnaissance commands or network scanning code exists.

dangerous_patterns.title: Drug names and medical terms misidentified as weak cryptographic algorithms
dangerous_patterns.description: 33 high-severity flags for weak cryptographic algorithms across all skill files. No cryptographic code exists in this project. The scanner triggered on medical abbreviations (MD, sha sequences in drug names) and the word 'hash' in content descriptions. These are false positives from pattern matching on clinical text.

content.user_title: Generate Professional SOAP Notes from Clinical Text
content.value_statement: Healthcare providers spend hours writing clinical documentation after patient encounters. This skill converts unstructured clinical notes into structured SOAP format in seconds, saving time while maintaining medical documentation standards.
actual_capabilities: Extract medical entities like symptoms, diagnoses, medications, and procedures from free-text clinical narratives
Classify clinical content into Subjective, Objective, Assessment, and Plan sections automatically
Parse and normalize temporal information including onset, duration, and progression of symptoms
Detect negated findings and uncertain statements to prevent documentation errors
Generate formatted SOAP notes in Markdown or JSON output formats
Process clinical text with rule-based NLP including vital sign extraction and pattern matching
limitations: Not a diagnostic tool and cannot make medical decisions or diagnoses independently
Optimized for English language clinical text with limited multilingual support
May lose context or accuracy in very long or highly complex clinical encounters
Cannot interpret non-verbal cues, tone of voice, or emphasis from audio input

use_cases.title: Primary Care Documentation
use_cases.description: Family physicians and internists convert consultation notes into structured SOAP format for efficient EHR entry after patient visits.

use_cases.title: Medical Education and Training
use_cases.description: Medical students and residents practice clinical documentation by comparing their SOAP notes against AI-generated examples from case studies.

use_cases.title: Clinical Research Data Extraction
use_cases.description: Researchers processing clinical narratives extract structured data from free-text patient encounters for retrospective studies.

prompt_templates.title: Basic SOAP note from short encounter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a SOAP note from this patient encounter: "45-year-old male with chest pain for 2 hours, radiating to left arm, BP 160/95, HR 105. Assessment: acute coronary syndrome."
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick documentation of a straightforward clinical encounter with clear symptoms and assessment.

prompt_templates.title: Structured note with negation and uncertainty flags
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert the following clinical dictation into a structured SOAP note with all four sections. Flag any negated findings and uncertain statements for physician review. Include extracted vital signs and medications with dosages. Patient encounter: [clinical text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard clinical documentation where you want the skill to highlight uncertain or negated findings for safety review.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-problem encounter with entity extraction
prompt_templates.prompt: Process this multi-problem clinical encounter into a comprehensive SOAP note. Extract all medical entities, vital signs, medications with dosages, and temporal information. Separate acute from chronic conditions in the assessment section. Include differential diagnoses where applicable. [clinical text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex patient with multiple active conditions requiring organized problem-list formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Full clinical narrative with ICD-10 and medication reconciliation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a complete SOAP note from this complex clinical narrative. Include ICD-10 compatible diagnoses where possible, medication reconciliation with dosages and frequencies, differential diagnoses ranked by likelihood, and a time-bound management plan with follow-up milestones. Flag all uncertain elements for mandatory physician review. Identify any missing information that would be needed for a complete note. [clinical text]
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive documentation suitable for specialist consultations or complex hospital admissions.

output_examples.input: Patient reports headache for 3 days, worse in the morning. No nausea or vision changes. BP 138/88.
output_examples.output:
**Subjective**: Patient reports 3-day history of headaches, worse in the morning. Denies associated nausea and vision changes.

**Objective**: Vital signs: BP 138/88 mmHg

**Assessment**: Tension-type headache, likely. No red flag features identified.

**Plan**: Conservative management with NSAIDs as needed. Increase hydration. Return if symptoms worsen or new neurological symptoms develop.

output_examples.input: 62-year-old female with diabetes here for follow-up. Home glucose readings 120-140. Taking metformin 1000mg twice daily. A1c improved from 7.8% to 7.2%.
output_examples.output:
**Subjective**: 62-year-old female with Type 2 DM presents for routine follow-up. Reports home glucose readings 120-140 mg/dL. Compliant with metformin 1000mg BID. Denies hypoglycemic episodes.

**Objective**: Hgb A1c 7.2% (improved from 7.8% at prior visit)

**Assessment**: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus, well controlled with improving glycemic control. Successful medication adherence and lifestyle modifications.

**Plan**: Continue metformin 1000mg PO BID. Recheck Hgb A1c in 3 months. Continue current diet and exercise regimen.

output_examples.input: 28-year-old twisted ankle playing soccer. Heard pop, immediate swelling, cannot bear weight. X-ray negative for fracture.
output_examples.output:
**Subjective**: 28-year-old female presents with acute right ankle injury sustained during soccer. Reports twisting mechanism with audible pop and immediate swelling. Pain rated 7/10. Unable to bear weight on affected limb. Denies numbness or tingling.

**Objective**: Significant swelling over lateral ankle. Tenderness over ATFL. X-ray negative for fracture. Distal pulses intact.

**Assessment**: Right ankle sprain, Grade 2. ATFL injury suspected.

**Plan**: RICE protocol with ace wrap compression. Crutches for ambulation. Ibuprofen 600mg PO q6h PRN pain. Orthopedics referral. Return in 1 week or sooner if symptoms worsen.

best_practices: Always provide clear, complete clinical text with symptoms, history, and examination findings for best extraction results
Review all AI-generated SOAP notes for clinical accuracy before use, especially the Assessment and Plan sections
Use the patient ID and provider fields to maintain proper documentation context and traceability in the output
anti_patterns: Do not use generated SOAP notes as the final patient record without mandatory physician review and approval
Do not input real patient identifiers in non-secure environments or when using the tool for testing purposes
Do not rely on this tool for diagnostic decisions, differential diagnosis selection, or treatment planning

faq.question: What is a SOAP note?
faq.answer: A SOAP note is a structured method of clinical documentation with four sections: Subjective (patient-reported symptoms and history), Objective (measurable findings like vital signs and lab results), Assessment (diagnosis and clinical impression), and Plan (treatment steps and follow-up). It is the standard format used in electronic health records.

faq.question: Can this skill replace a human medical scribe?
faq.answer: No. This skill assists with documentation workflow but cannot replace human clinical judgment. All generated SOAP notes require review and approval by a licensed healthcare provider before entering any patient record.

faq.question: What types of clinical encounters work best with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill performs best with internal medicine, family practice, and primary care encounters. Highly specialized fields like neurosurgery or rare disease clinics may have lower entity extraction accuracy due to domain-specific terminology.

faq.question: Does this skill comply with medical privacy regulations like HIPAA?
faq.answer: The skill runs locally and does not transmit data to external servers. However, users are responsible for de-identifying protected health information and following their institution's privacy and security policies when using the tool.

faq.question: What output formats are available for the generated SOAP notes?
faq.answer: The skill supports Markdown format for human review and documentation, and JSON format for system integration or further processing. Both formats preserve the structured SOAP section organization.

faq.question: Can this skill process clinical text in languages other than English?
faq.answer: The skill is optimized for English-language clinical text. It may partially process other languages but entity extraction and section classification accuracy will be significantly reduced for non-English input.
